sections.title: Part 1
sections.passage: You will hear a conversation between a customer and a representative from Easy Life Cleaning Services.

questionGroups.title: Easy Life Cleaning Services
questionGroups.instructions: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD for each answer.

content.text:
**Basic cleaning package offered**
● Cleaning all surfaces
● Cleaning the

content.question: Cleaning the ______ throughout the apartment

content.text:
throughout the apartment
● Cleaning shower, sinks, toilet etc.

**Additional services agreed**
● Every week
  - Cleaning the

content.question: Cleaning the ______

content.text: - Ironing clothes -

content.question: Ironing clothes - ______ only

content.text:
only
● Every month
  - Cleaning all the

content.question: Cleaning all the ______ from the inside

content.text:
from the inside
  - Washing down the

content.question: Washing down the ______

content.text:
**Other possibilities**
● They can organise a plumber or an

content.question: They can organise a plumber or an ______ if necessary.

content.text:
if necessary.
● A special cleaning service is available for customers who are allergic to

content.question: A special cleaning service is available for customers who are allergic to ______

content.text:
**Information on the cleaners**
● Before being hired, all cleaners have a background check carried out by the

content.question: All cleaners have a background check carried out by the ______

content.text:
● References are required.
● All cleaners are given

content.question: All cleaners are given ______ for two weeks.

content.text:
for two weeks.
● Customers send a

content.question: Customers send a ______ after each visit.

content.text:
after each visit.
● Usually, each customer has one regular cleaner.

sections.title: Part 2
sections.passage: You will hear a talk about staff turnover in the hotel industry.

questionGroups.instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.question: Many hotel managers are unaware that their staff often leave because of
What is the impact of high staff turnover on managers?
What mistake should managers always avoid?

options.text: neglecting to have enough staff during busy periods

questions.question: What unexpected benefit did Dunwich Hotel notice after improving staff retention rates?

options.text: an increase in loyalty club membership
a rise in spending per customer

questionGroups.instructions: Which way of reducing staff turnover was used in each of the following hotels? Write the correct letter, A, B or C, next to Questions 15-20.

sections.title: Part 3
sections.passage: You will hear two students, Thomas and Jeanne, discussing Thomas's sporting activities and the development of sporting equipment.

questionGroups.question: Which TWO points do Thomas and Jeanne make about Thomas's sporting activities at school?

options.text: He should have felt more positive about them.
The training was too challenging for him.
He could have worked harder at them.
His parents were disappointed in him.

questionGroups.question: Which TWO feelings did Thomas experience when he was in Kenya?

questionGroups.instructions: What comment do the students make about the development of each of the following items of sporting equipment? Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-H, next to Questions 25-30.
questionGroups.optionsTitle: Comments about the development of the equipment

options.text: The material was being mass produced for another purpose.
People often needed to make their own.
It often had to be replaced.
No one using it liked it at first.

sections.title: Part 4
sections.passage: You will hear a lecture about maple syrup.

questionGroups.title: Maple syrup
questionGroups.instructions: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

content.text:
**What is maple syrup?**
● made from the sap of the maple tree
● colour described as

content.question: colour described as ______

content.text:
● added to food or used in cooking
● very

content.question: very ______ compared to refined sugar

content.text:
compared to refined sugar

**The maple tree**
● has many species
● needs moist soil but does not need fertiliser as well
● needs sunny days and cool nights
● best growing conditions and

content.question: best growing conditions and ______ are in Canada and North America

content.text:
are in Canada and North America
● maple leaf has been on the Canadian flag since 1964

**Early maple sugar producers**
● made holes in the tree trunks
● used tree bark to make containers for collection
● used hot

content.question: used hot ______ to heat the sap

content.text:
to heat the sap
● sweetened food and drink with sugar

**Today's maple syrup**

**The trees**
● Tree trunks may not have the correct

content.question: Tree trunks may not have the correct ______ until they have been growing for 40 years.

content.text:
until they have been growing for 40 years.
● The changing temperature and movement of water within the tree produces the sap.

**The production**
● A tap is drilled into the trunk and a

content.question: A tap is drilled into the trunk and a ______ carries the sap into a bucket.

content.text:
carries the sap into a bucket.
● Large pans of sap called evaporators are heated by means of a

content.question: Large pans of sap called evaporators are heated by means of a ______

content.text: ● A lot of

content.question: A lot of ______ is produced during the evaporation process.

content.text:
is produced during the evaporation process.
● 'Sugar sand' is removed because it makes the syrup look

content.question: 'Sugar sand' is removed because it makes the syrup look ______ and affects the taste.

content.text:
and affects the taste.
● The syrup is ready for use.
● A huge quantity of sap is needed to make a

content.question: A huge quantity of sap is needed to make a ______ of maple syrup.

content.text: of maple syrup.
